mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% as of March 2010 indicates post-crisis labor market recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero signals extreme monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and increases asset allocation flows into equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of March 2010 indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with expanding housing starts indicates accelerating consumer demand and construction activity, which signals robust aggregate demand and positive earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363194M and HOUST at 575K units indicate post-crisis demand recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns fail to show significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) when both RSAFS and HOUST are rising simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rising month-over-month signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side productivity gains, which support profit margins and positive equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.61 as of March 2010 shows recovery from crisis lows
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals sustained labor market tightening that increases wage pressure and consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through higher consumption and earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129750K and UNRATE at 9.7% indicate labor market stabilization
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with employment gains
failure_condition: Interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) when PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline occur jointly.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI remaining subdued while monetary policy is accommodative signals that real interest rates are negative, which compresses discount rates and increases equity valuations, producing negative forward returns if inflation expectations rise sharply, reversing the mechanism.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.729 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% indicate low inflation with extreme accommodation
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation risk
failure_condition: Interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when inflation accelerates amid zero rates.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly signals underlying economic expansion strength; when combined with rising retail sales, it confirms broad-based demand acceleration that supports equity valuations and positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns fail to show significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) when both GDPC1 growth and RSAFS increases are present.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential demand destruction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth concerns emerge.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.61 as of March 2010 provides baseline for detecting deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
